description: Generic rigid-body contact causality capability for active-to-passive motion transfer across balls, pins, crates, or other contact-driven actors. Destruction/fracture should use a separate thresholded fracture capability.
physical_assumptions: Active bodies may receive structured initial velocity, impulse, or action trace.
Passive bodies must start with zero unexplained motion unless explicitly marked as active.
Passive body motion must be explainable by a prior or same-frame contact event.
Collision graph edges must reference known physics-critical objects.
verifier_rules: Passive objects have near-zero initial velocity.
Passive displacement before first causal contact is below threshold.
Expected contact graph edges appear in contact events.
repair_suggestions: Move hidden passive velocity into active impulse or contact response.
Ensure all passive colliders are collision-enabled.
Export contact events from the physics backend.
Record active/passive roles in case_spec and runtime artifacts.
